title: All Fields in Exception Classes Should be Final
description: Fields in an exception class should be declared as final. This enforces immutability, making the class easier to reason about. Furthermore, it ensures that the application's state at the point of error remains constant.
recommendation: Declare all fields in the exception class as final. Initialize these fields during the object creation.
impact: Ensures class fields do not change after initialization, representing an accurate state of the application at the point of error.

checks.message: All fields in exception classes should be final.
